commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OAuth refresh coordination to key Codex profiles by account, email, or refresh-token fingerprint, adopt fresh same-group credentials, and adds Codex OAuth duplicate-profile helper tests.

Reproducibility: yes. via source inspection, though I did not run a failing current-main test: current main keys both the in-process queue and file lock by provider plus profileId, so duplicated same-account Codex profiles can refresh independently. The PR body also supplies after-fix terminal proof for the coordinated path.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can address the CodeQL token-fingerprint alert without changing the core OAuth coordination behavior.

target.title: fix: coordinate Codex OAuth refreshes by account
target.body:
## Summary
- Coordinate Codex OAuth refreshes by account/email/refresh-token fingerprint instead of only profile ID.
- Reuse fresh same-account credentials before retrying a stale rotating refresh token.
- Add a secret-safe Codex OAuth plan helper that emits metadata and short hashes only.

## Why
Dolboda hit `401 refresh_token_reused` when duplicated/stale OpenAI Codex OAuth profiles existed across OpenClaw agents/profiles. The previous lock was too narrow and could still allow same-account refresh attempts to reuse a rotated token.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-coordination.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-codex-plan.test.ts` — passed, 52/52 tests.
- `pnpm check:changed` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- Static scan for hardcoded secret/shell/eval/pickle/SQL patterns — 0 findings.
- Independent review gate passed after fixing the same-process re-entrant file-lock race.

## Safety
- No Claude/Anthropic/fallback behavior added.
- No raw access/refresh token output added; refresh-token fingerprints use SHA-256.
- No live Dolboda deploy/restart/reauth performed by this PR.


## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: OpenClaw Codex OAuth refresh coordination now serializes same-account/different-profile refreshes before they hit the re-entrant file lock, so a second stale duplicate profile does not reuse the already-rotated refresh token.
- **Real environment tested**: Dolboda client box, OpenClaw branch `dolboda-codex-oauth-hardening-pr` at `cf1c30641c`, Node 22.22.0, real OpenClaw auth-profile store/file-lock code with a temporary local OpenClaw state directory. No live gateway deploy/restart/reauth.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `cd /home/dev/worktrees/dolboda-codex-oauth-hardening-pr && node --import tsx .artifacts/codex-oauth-real-proof.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the real local OpenClaw setup:

```text
refresh_call_1: accountId=acct-proof-shared profile_refresh_suffix=refresh-proof-value
refresh_calls_while_first_blocked=1
[agents/auth-profiles] adopted fresh OAuth credential from same refresh coordination group
final_refresh_calls=1
result_access_equal=true
resultA_access=rotated-access-proof-value
resultB_access=rotated-access-proof-value
```

- **Observed result after fix**: While the first same-account refresh was deliberately blocked, the second profile stayed behind the coordination queue (`refresh_calls_while_first_blocked=1`). After release, both profiles received the same rotated credential and only one refresh happened (`final_refresh_calls=1`).
- **What was not tested**: Live Dolboda gateway deploy/restart/reauth was intentionally not performed in this PR.
target.author: dbdbdipdip
